description: Apollo 9 Flight Journal: Day 1: Orbit 5 - Daylight Star Check

tokens: We are about to lose you here.
We will pick you up over Tananarive at 51.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It says here, "Open full control simulation panel, close the EVAP water control secondary valve."
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You might try a urine dump.
We're coming up on that time; it's near the terminator.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know, that's really sort of fun.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How's that for a position at the auto optics?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Wonder if I rolled stomach-down if I can take a leak.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You might; it might be psychological.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Mine's psychological, too, and I've tried every position.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughter) I can't - I can't find the psychological --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I bet we have to go down and grab a hold of something first.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, something with a little handle on the left.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's funny, I didn't have much trouble taking the first one.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, well, I guess if it hurts bad enough, I'll pee.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you don't get kidney stones, first.
Oh, did you get the tunnel?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, Jim, you really drank a lot of potable water.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, gees, listen, I ate - Well, it was almost undrinkable.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I think we need to get some bags out.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Somebody has got to stay here and drive the boat, though, don't they?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, here, can I do it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, why don't you just stuff the food up here and --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I've got to get through all this folderol to do this.
I'll tell you, that's a good place to hold your checklist because something keeps getting in the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, no, it's this screw up there that I keep banging into.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Here, let me attack this problem.
How am I going to get this thing off?
What am I going to do with it when I get it off?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Where do we start with food?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, you guys turn your heads.
Maybe I can take a leak.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I feel like my balls are asleep.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You got me interested in going, and now I just went, and I think I wet on myself.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's what I've been afraid of.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: My poor talleywhacker's shriveled all up, I've been so frightened here on this.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The food is right behind them, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, did we have any place in here to eat today?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I don't know if we did or not.
Would you believe, eat period (garbled) 5 hours long.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: But we were lucky that we got it done.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, we - we could have got it done, there's just no doubt about that --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, we had a - we had a really -
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: -- (garbled) louse up a lot of fuel.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, we had a couple of real fine problems that got right in the middle of there, that took a lot of time.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That docking would have gone bang, just like --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, I - Oh, I meant - this other thing here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I - I'll tell you, I think --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: -- I think, you did a brilliant job on that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I bet that was a terrible feeling --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Gee, I was really, really feeling awful --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, look at that beautiful horizon!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: -- I was figuring I hadn't got you guys in the (missing text) and I was about ready to just lose you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Even if you hadn't - shit, oh dear.
That's the breaks of the ball game, David, and you either do it or you don't --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's what I get paid for --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, the Earth is concave up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Rusty, here's your day 1, meal B.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to give everybody a toothbrush, now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, gee, what are we going to do with that?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: What are we going to do with that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm going to leave my teeth dirty.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Rusty, I'm going to give you the red toothbrush.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I get the red one.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm going to give Rusty the blue one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Shit, I can't figure out what - what to do with my checklist, and other valuable items like that.
I can only (garbled) Should we unsuit tonight and go to bed in the other suit?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's going to take 2 days to get unsuited.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, Jim doesn't want his toothbrush, which means I've got to carry it for him, so I will.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, you don't have to carry it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I don't know what else to do with it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Will that thing go through my neck?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm going to find some obscure piece of Velcro to put that toothbrush on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: All we're going to have with that toothbrush is a loose toothbrush in here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm going to get - I get three food packs out, okay?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, shoot, I want one, too.
Well, they both should be around there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, one for each of us.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, and then I'll put this thing back.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Shit, I'm going to take these pockets off, too.
I don't know what I'm going to do with those things either.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Where do you stow your pocket?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, you can find a place.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, your toothbrush will be the marker.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Where am I trying to go, anyway?
I tell you, I'm just going to - I'm just going to drift right on over there, and I'm not going to use a - a thing but pulse, and I'm going to just end up there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You have that written down or something, Jim, somewhere?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Does anybody remember what we've done so far today?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I tried to log some things, but -
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I want to get on that - that Sony and get that T&D started.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hell, that's not a very good spot; it hides the clock.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Maybe we ought to put that Y-Y strut out again, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I can - I can reach it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Why do we want to put the Y-Y strut out again?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, so it doesn't jiggle back and forth.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I get nervous listening to it jiggle.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We can't do the daylight star check if we're looking at the Earth, can we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That attitude takes care of that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I wonder if it takes care of us for the whole time?
Oh, look, we're - Heck, we're posigrade again.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: So one-half of a rev[olution] from right now, puts us - facing the sky.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, except we have to do a 90-degree pitch (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What are we doing with trash?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm sure going to be passing a lot of trash here, while I make supper - or breakfast, or whatever we're eating.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Damn, I have just got to take a leak.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Rusty, maybe if you - you stood up.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Jim, I've tried - I'm in the same boat.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you get the urine dump system out and try that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (Laughter) You know - That might be a good idea.
Suck your tinker out the hole.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to dump mine, so I can get more room in it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm afraid to take one, because when I do, I'll overflow.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hope I can reach those scissors.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: What's the time - what's the sunrise time?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, I can - I can give you an estimate (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, it is - it is on the flight plan.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: What was the time for that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: 07:05, which is on the flight plan.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It says, "GET start, sunrise - Oh, sunrise minus 15."
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Do we have to be there then?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Then, I'm going to have to hurry faster.
I'm going to have to - shoot - waste some fuel there.
Are those things firing out there?
Boy, they really light up the sky, don't they?
And I've got to go 110 degrees, yet.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, I'm going to get something to eat before I die.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I want something with some liquid in it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We'll, we've got a lot of basic housekeeping yet to do.
We're supposed to go to sleep in an hour.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: God, you know it's almost a quarter to 6?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, Lu, get that up for 06:30, babe.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Steak, salmon salad, cocoa, chicken and gravy.
I got to make that stuff.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, hey, you know what we're supposed to have?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: They're up there by Rusty's head.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, I think I'll have one of those.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, I think I will, too.
Let's start, well, you know, gradually.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here, give me one of those other meals.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Here, let me see if I can stow some things here down there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I've got the white one.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What - what - what color is mine?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I gave you the three first bags I found.
I hope those are the right ones.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, but I could sure use some (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, mine's - I - I - I opened the red one.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Doesn't make any difference; (garbled) we're all starting the same.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, there's the Big Dipper over there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I'll be looking in here, when we get there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We won't be looking at Polaris.
We'll be looking at - just above (garbled) (garbled) Alphecca.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm not getting very far as far as (garbled) --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, that airglow is really prominent.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Obviously, you don't have to be a genius to figure out it's there (laughter).
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Three dummy astronauts can figure that out almost every day.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me see, I got 9 minutes before I got to get - adaptate to -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, Dave, I'm not going to be there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I'm sorry - I mean - I (garbled) That's okay.
Listen, let me explain this to you.
I just read up on it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'd like to know what you're trying to do because I got to -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got to begin dark adaptation at 40 - 15 minutes prior to sunrise.
And then I count the stars at sunrise.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, you're supposed to be in that attitude, huh?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: At sunrise, I got to be in the attitude, but I don't have to be - We don't have to be there at 15 minutes prior, because I just focus - close my eyes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: -- 7 - 7 is when I have to be there - 7 o'clock.
And I've got to go - pitch (garbled) I got to go (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Think I'm going to take the Sony into the (garbled) when I go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, you don't even - can't even see a change - I'm - I'm on the 1-degree-per-second scale here, and I fired 10 to 12 pulses here and it doesn't even wiggle the needle.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know - you know something I'm beginning to realize?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: All this time, I've been playing around in the simulator --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: -- docked, you've been in the LM.
I've seen it in the simulator.
It's - That's the way it is in the simulator.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, how about the water gun?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: What are we going to - what are we supposed to do with these?
I guess we're supposed to wad up the garbage, and put it back where we got it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's see if the Sony is recording at 6 hours 41 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim, you got the water gun?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, I haven't; I'll get it for you in just a minute.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Stow this crap, so I'll know about where it is.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What do I do with all - Oh, you wanted the water gun, that's right.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Maybe if I put water in the bag, maybe it will make me think of leaking.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I think it will, but let's see.
Start thinking about emptying my bag, here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, what is this supposed to be, half-ounce squirts?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's see, (garbled) 6 minutes I think I'll do - talk about the T&D - (garbled) want to talk about the T&D (garbled) Yes.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, I wonder if we ought to try the hot water.
Hey, we - we never turned on the hot water.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: -- It started out just about as planned.
We pitched around, and when we got around facing the S-IVB, we were directly lined up and just exactly right according to the angles on the ball, although we seemed to be a little bit high in pitch.
We did - we did some pitchup to get the - the needle, and it looked like we were lined up relative to the COAS.
And the vent seemed to be somewhat more than we expected; the S-IVB started closing, and I had about 0.6 of a foot per second in at separation, and when we turned around, I never took any out.
And the S-IVB closed the gap, naturally.
I guess we were something like 50 feet away when we turned around and were facing it.

In the process of trying to get lined up with the target, we noticed there was no left translation, and we started drifting over some to the right; and I backed off to keep clearance, for the S-IVB to continue to close, and I tried to find the problem.
And after a while, it became apparent there definitely was no left translation.
And we cycled the switches for RCS auto control and changed A/C roll all up to Bus A; all the rest were on Bus B, and nothing seemed to work there.
We got no translation in SCS rate command or any control mode, for that matter.
We drifted out to our right of the S-IVB, and I yawed left to try and get back in by translating forward which put us across the front of the S-IVB to the left side.

And about that time, figured out the problem and discovered that the quad C primary and secondary propellent isolation valves were talkback, talkbacks were barber pole, and that the quad B secondary isolation valve talkback was barber pole.
And another thing we had tried prior to discovering the talkbacks was the DAP, to see if that might be a problem, and it seemed to be working alright except for the translation to the left.

Back to the talkbacks, when Jim closed the isolation valve - or opened the isolation valves and the talkbacks were gray, we definitely knew - got a definite translation capability to the left, and went to CMC hold and realigned with the COAS, and on the target on the S-IVB.
And about the time we got realigned visually, the needle had preset, with a Verb 62; we were lined up almost exactly, which showed that the preflight-calculated angles were just right.
And we needed no closing velocity; the S-IVB closed the gap with the venting.
And we proceeded on in, and contacted it at approximately 0.3 foot per second, and capture occurred immediately, and there were no postcontact dynamics to speak of.

It drifted up a little bit and yawed left a little bit, which would have been an apparent pitchdown, yaw left to get it centered; and we went to CMC Free on contact, and it seemed to damp out the position, misaligned by a couple of degrees in pitch and about 1 in yaw.
I attempted to align with the translational thrusters, just minimum impulse, and it didn't seem to do much good.
So, I went to SCS minimum impulse and the rotational controller and that seemed to work alright, aligning the COAS to the target.

And the one thing that was noticeable: with the COAS full bright, it was very difficult to see the luminated reticle against the bright target background, and by this time, the sun was pretty much over our shoulders and there was - the target was very bright.
Once we got aligned with the - the target, the standoff cross, why we initiated retraction, and it took 8 to 10 seconds to retract.

The vehicles went straight in, and it sounded like we got two sets of latches in almost very - in a very short time in between, but it sounded like you could hear two groups of latches going, and dynamics was very slight.
Felt like a good solid line.
I guess that was about the size of it.
Jim, you want to go through what you saw?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't we - why don't we all go through what -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, there's a nice little (garbled) that just came of
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: How do you get the gas out of these bags, I wonder.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, would you stick this under the hot water and give me a shot or two --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want me to try to fix that for you?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's not running and it's not - You got to push the red button right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (garbled) I'm going to have to (garbled) move it slow.

Well, let's see, there's hardly anything else I can - there's very little I can add to what Dave has already said except that when we extended the probe, it only took a - just a second or so for it to get out.
There was a definite thunk, and the talkbacks changed, and there was a - every indication that it had gone out immediately.
When we came back in, I was watching the talkbacks, and they both changed to gray, at - at the same time, which would indicate that all the latches were close together.
When we checked the tunnel, we found all the latches had secured and everything looks okay up to there.
There didn't seem to be any problem I didn't get a chance to - to examine the - the drogues, to see if they had any damage.
I don't think (garbled) we were in a little bit of a hurry.
And I couldn't see up there very well at the time; we'll get to that tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Give it to Rusty and we'll have that all here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't think I have anything on it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, this has really got the gas in here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, now I got to start stopping this mother.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, looks like the coloration would be a little better.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Here, let me try one in yaw and see what happens.
Oh, man, and that really wiggles, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm about 15 degrees in pitch, Dave, and about 25 degrees in yaw; 70 degrees off in roll.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That chicken and gravy would be good with hot water.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, do you have any hot water?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I need 3 ounces if you got time.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: How much does a squirt give?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's either a half or a whole.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: This is Apollo 9 here, reading you loud and clear.
Houston, how do you read Apollo 9?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, that comm is going to have to get better, or we're not going to do any rendezvous.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Looks like they sealed the bag on the other side of the line.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's see, one squirt is how much, you think?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's either a half of an ounce, or a whole ounce.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: How much do you put in this bag, Rusty?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Three - I'll give you three squirts.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm afraid it'll melt the bag.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, I'm going to let you look at it and tell me how you like it, because I'm going to have to turn the lights down here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Rusty, I'm going to count stars, so maybe you could remember the number for me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, you want me to (garbled) 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, or mark, mark, mark, mark, or what?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, I'll count the number and give you a number.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'll count four - I'll give you four numbers per quadrant, okay?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When are you going to start?
Oh, you're not going to start yet.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I'm not; I'm not going to cheat now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, that really wiggles that thing.
I wonder what that stroking test is going to feel like.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm going to run the DSKY here to line the optics up, Jim, in case you want to --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Leave unto tomorrow the evils thereof.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I bet I know what's wrong on the zero.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Apollo 9, this is Houston through Tananarive.
We're probably not getting you here.
We got about another minute and a half, and if you can read me, we'll see you over Guam at about 17.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: This is Apollo 9, and we're reading you loud and clear through Tananarive.
We'll look for you over Guam.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, we're getting you in here now.
I didn't read you at all the first time or two around.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, I heard your call a couple of times but I guess we just weren't getting down to you.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It hasn't been too stern here off Tananarive today.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're just taking a little time out to eat here right now.
We haven't had anything to eat yet, so, that's a - If you wonder what we're doing, that's it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, our plan is that as we come over Guam and back across the States, why we'll discuss all our system problems and so forth before you go to sleep tonight.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I got bad news for them.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Are we at the attitude, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, can somebody fix me a Tang or something?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Although, I don't dare put anymore in the bright end.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And we speak Sayonara at Tananarive.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Oh, boy, does that taste good.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Laughter) We're just going to leave Dave down there and -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're right at the pitch attitude, Dave, I've got - 30 degrees to go on roll.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, it shouldn't be bad all the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But really yaw is turning into pitch now, so -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, are those stars bright out there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, wonder if I can see the Magellanic Clouds?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, that's what I was thinking.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Have you had chicken and gravy up yet, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I'm eating it right now.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, I can't even - I don't even - I'm in the 1-degree-per-second scale, here, and I can't even make it move.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You got to be patient, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I am, but I'm going in the wrong direction.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know, it's better - One - one acceleration pulse will - give you the same rate as 50 minimum impulses.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Lord, give me patience and hurry up (laughter).
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (Laughter) Don't make me laugh; I'm eating.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That Moon is going to be right -
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, we're up to roll attitude, and we're just about 3 or 4 degrees from pitch attitude, and --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, Dave, in the daytime, I bet the Moon will go maybe it will.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: What about - You got to pitch down?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, to you, it looks like I'm going to have to pitch down about another 10 degrees.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, well, it'll be alright, then.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, at 3 minutes, I'll just go ahead and stop it wherever we are.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's see, you got to pitch down there, (garbled) the Moon's right on the edge of the field of view right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'm only going to pitch down another 6 degrees.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: If you do that, we'll be alright.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, now, are we going to be (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We have to use a little tablet, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Have I pitched down far enough?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What I should do now is yaw left about 5 degrees.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, that would be just about - just about right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I wouldn't sweat it though, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I stopped the pitch motion now.
Now, I'm just going to let what I - my yaw that I had in there going on over.
Now I got about 0.000001 degree per second.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: David, I'm going to make you a drink if you're -
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, (garbled) almost time right now, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, about 45 seconds and it's going to be sunrise.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: In 45 seconds, tell me and I'll start time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Tell me if - if - it is about 10 seconds to go.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, the LM is starting to get lighter.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Jimmy, can you handle some trash, here?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We really ought to wait until sunrise.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, let's do it on the - on the - on the time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Excuse me, Dave, you got awhile - ways to wait yet.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, you got 40 seconds more - That's about right.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're rotating, or rolling, or something.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, close your eyes for just a minute, Dave, and I'll give it one little shot here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, now, it's just - it's really zapping right off of the quadrant.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, the daylight star check, started out about 10 seconds prior to the official sunrise, and counted through to about 19 stars, the last few of which were in the Big Dipper - Well, the Big Dipper is the last one to remain visible starting from the upper right quadrant, going counterclockwise.
When the sun came up, it filled the inside of the quad, and one of the jets on the LM quad that sticks down, the one that points to about - it's like about minus X.
And as soon as that filled up with light, it washed out everything but a couple or three stars and the Big Dipper.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can still see some, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I can still see a few stars, and right now it's how late, Rusty?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can still see a couple of stars, and the Big Dipper.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And another thing that was noticeable: the Moon was probably about 4 or 5 degrees above the field of view, and it reflected on the prism, on the telescope, and gave a light gray band right across the center.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, GET plus 5; I've got to do it again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, that's going to be at - What time now - That's --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's see what - I wonder what - It was 07:04:45 was the first one.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, so this one's going to be 07:09:45.
Okay, you got - you got over 2 minutes, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'll give you your cocoa, here, if you want to make some hot cocoa.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to cut the bottom for you.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Don't lose my dark - I'm going to just keep my eyes closed.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I've got it all ready, so - I was just feeling sorry for you.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I's going to make me some salmon salad, now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I haven't gotten to it, yet.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know what I need is something to drink before I die.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: All I get out of there is air.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know, the hot water didn't put in - as near as much gas as the gun does.
I bet the cold water is better down there, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I wonder if this plastic on this thing will melt - Oh, (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You've got just about a minute, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to shoot a little pulse here, David.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Can you even the needles, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Probably got to pack these things with - with as little bit of air in them as possible, too --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: -- so that when we depressurize this thing, we don't have them all inflate.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Give me a mark, (garbled) when you get there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What kind of a star check is that?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (garbled) trying to figure out how to make this thing (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: On the -5 minutes after sunrise, got the horizon and no - no stars at all, and the quads are now very bright.
Got a - the cloud cover - it seems the Earth is mostly blue --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: -- with (laughter) white clouds, but it's not a dense cover of white clouds.

(garbled) and the sky is black, and there are no stars visible at all, and I can still see the prism split - the reflection on the prism split, which gives me a light band across the center of the field of view.
And I guess that's about the size of it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I got that down in the record (garbled) Hey, you're supposed to have horizon in here, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, guess I'll put it down (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I have it; I have it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Say, did you cut the end off for him?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I guess we ought to look at the flight plan, and see what else we're supposed to be doing here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Weren't we supposed to change one of those lithium hydroxide canisters or something?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We need - we need to get the big flight plan out, I guess.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: -- filter change, number 1; we missed it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, if there's a smile on my face, it's because I just took a leak.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (Laughter) Dave, would you also fill mine up for me when you get -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It didn't do too bad last time, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I got - I don't have that much air in that one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Maybe you just have to get the head off of it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I guess I might as well eat some dry things here.
Toasted breadcubes or sugar cookie cubes?
I guess I'll save them for dessert.
You know, no matter which way I look, it feels like it's down.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: How did your stuff come out, ounce-wise --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, did we ever get the cabin vent open again down there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, my God, let's open the cabin vent.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, it's alright; we were going to close it at noon -- at night.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, why don't we open her?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I have the LM as holding at zero.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, how did your water come out ounce-wise, on those two things I have you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, mine came out good, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Would you squirt mine full when you get a chance down there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: -- let me get my - some tape on these optics, so I don't lose them.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, does this food taste good.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You know, the thing that impresses me most is how quiet it is.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: What are we doing with the cabin fan?
I think they're both off, aren't they?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Turn one to see what it sounds like.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Supposed to start our rest period in less than 2 hours - an hour and 45 minutes.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: They forgot a few things in here, like taking your suit off, and putting it back on, and -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Dave, you want some more food?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, let me get - I haven't got what I got, yet.
I'm busy as a little beaver.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Gee whiz, we don't have a thing to do until -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, Dave's got to change that filter.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I will; just hang on, gang.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, no, I'm not rushing you.
I just want to - I just don't want to forget it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let me have that water gun.
Man, I need some - something to drink.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, Jim, I'll get yours now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, man, that's a load of air.
Man, I'll tell you, this toasted bread cru - cubes never tasted better.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Guam.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We would like to have P00 and Accept, please.
We are going to give you a state vector.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Can you talk a few minutes here?
We are going to have you over Guam for about 5 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, stand by one just a second.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: What I want to talk about is that X-[axis] PIPA bias.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, we will take that one first.
We are showing an error in that X-[axis] PIPA of about 0.04 feet per second squared.
The plan is to not do anything with that tonight, and we will update that tomorrow prior to the first burn.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Is it within the tolerance of what you can update?
transcript.speaker: Unknown

tokens: Okay, that takes care of that.
I would just like to ask a fast question.
I assume that you have no reading on that SCS helium pressure that's still gone.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: That is affirmative, and still reading full scale low.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Another item on this master alarm on the hard docking.
We don't have you a good explanation; however, we do have some info in from the Cape that this was found on spacecraft 106 when they docked, and they hadn't found out why.
But they did get an unexplained master alarm when they docked down there with 106.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And we are going to replay the data when you dock to see if we can get anything out of it but we can not close the loop on that one at this time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Do you have any idea what could have caused our primary and secondary propellant valves to go closed?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I think you must be looking at my sheet here, Jim, because that was my exact item coming up next I would like to ask you.
We feel that two explanations, one was a stray electrical current there that actually did it, or do you feel that you could have bumped the switches when you were changing seats?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: No, I don't think so because, I don't think we could have bumped them because we did an RCS check after that, and it was dark in here but I looked through all of the quads and I looked at all the talkbacks.
It is possible but not very probable that I missed all three of those talkbacks.
I was wondering if we couldn't have had the jolt from the separation between the service module and the SLA caused them to go closed.
I can't imagine that we would only have one of the talkbacks on the D-quad go closed for any other reason.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, that was something we wanted to verify - that the talkback that was closed on quad Delta was the secondary propellant.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: C had primary and secondary closed, D or Delta had just the secondary closed.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, we copy that, and we agree with you.
We are really at a loss how the secondary propellant only talkback could have gotten in that condition.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: So that is something that we will have to think about here over the night.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Be advised of one other thing.
Sort of keep track of the venting - cabin vent.
We didn't go back to waste the vent overboard until 07:15.
We didn't get that open again until then.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And you know when we closed it, it was just prior to the docking.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Next item is, I would just like to - We are closing this one out about that LM power cycling.
That is running, as I mentioned before, just exactly on the cycle that we would expect and the way it was doing on the PAD.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, we have got some other things.
We will pick them up here over Hawaii at about 32.
I have a minute left, and I have a nav[igation] check to go with the state vector just passed you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We are going to have to sort through the food bags for a piece of paper.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Go ahead with the nav check.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, Apollo 9, You went over the hill with everything confirmed except the altitude, and we will see you over Hawaii.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Houston, did you get the readback?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You got it there, Jim - Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, it's the wrong sign on the latitude - on the longitude.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I might have kicked something down here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, listen, are we about time to take suits off, and all that stuff?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, if we're going to sleep with them off, we're going to have to start taking them off.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want to sleep with them off, you can go ahead and start taking them off.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What are you going to do?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's a pain in the can to get them off.
I wonder if I - I might not just keep it on tonight.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, here's another card; here's your S-IVB, (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Would you believe that even the salmon salad tastes good?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, let's see, what do we have else to do?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Vent the batteries, terminate the cabin purge; (garbled) we just got it started.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I got a real problem trying to pee, you know that?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, I'll tell you, it took me a long while, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Maybe if you took your suit off, it may help, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know, I might just do that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Some of my chocolate got away.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Gee, and we've got to get those napkins and all that stuff out, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I think I will take my suit off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, that ought to be interesting.
Here goes David for an hour.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: If he's not doing P52's, he's under the seat.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Who's making that racket down there?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Sounds like it's right under my couch.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Man, this blue suit's stable, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: See if we got any perigee torquing now there.
We're headed towards perigee, right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Wonder what this blue stuff is.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I want some water or something to drink.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Man, you really going at it, aren't you?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want a hand with anything?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Here, you want a hand, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, hey, you had your watchband on.
That might have had something to do with it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I hope the girls have got the champagne out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, that thing sure doesn't refill very fast, does it?
I'd like to get that fifth tank filled up.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I didn't get to confirm all your nav checkout.
If you run it, you have probably discovered the sign was wrong on the longitude.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Oh, we're glad you knew that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I guess the - Did the rest of it go okay?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And are you free to talk now?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: What we would like to have you do at this time is turn off fans - the fans and heaters in both H tanks.
And want to let the pressure drop down to 200 [psi, 1380 kPa] and then have you manually maintain that at 200 until you power down.

And after you're powered down, just before sacking out, we are going to turn on the fan in H tank 1, and the estimates on this one is that it will slowly build up the pressure and when you wake up in the morning it will have built back up to 235 [psi, 1620 kPa] and it will keep the master alarm from coming on through the night.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, Are we squared, away on that, Apollo 9?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, you want us to turn the heaters and fans off on both the H tanks, and when do you want us to do that, now?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can do that right now.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We would also - Have you started a charge on battery B?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Negative, we weren't going to start the charge until we went to sleep.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We will go ahead and agree with that, Apollo 9.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're going - You're going to call us and tell us to turn it on just before we go to sleep.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and this O flow high readings you were getting - We consider that a closed item.
How you feel on this one, Apollo 9?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I think it is a closed item also.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, and on Rusty's comment on SPS-1, our data shows that both ball valves opened right the money - opened together.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We may have just had a sticky gauge in the cockpit.
How about PICON valves that we have on the quantity gauge?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: This one we will have to look at some more.
We don't believe that it is a valid reading at this time, Apollo 9.
That - On that short of a burn, we feel that the PUGS worked for such a short time that it probably didn't get a valid reading, and we don't believe that.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And on SPS-1 everything - It was a nominal burn.
GNC is real happy; the PC and everything else looks real good, so that - Looks like we are in fine shape on it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, we are about to lose you here for about a couple of minutes and we will see you over the Redstone about 38.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We should have you through to Redstone now.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through the Redstone.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You are weak but clear, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You are coming in clear, Here.
We would like to have you go back to block on your CM telemetry,
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Let me ask you a question about the other H tank.
If we run them both down to 200 [psi, 1380 kPa], and we turn the fan on in H tank number 1, what are we going to do with tank number 2?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I copied that; would you stand by one?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Copy your question, and what we're - What we're saying is that the pressure will stay equal in tank 2 just due to the heat leak, even though we are feeding primarily out of tank 1, but that pressure should come up right along with tank 1.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And also we would like - Could you verify that the surge tank is on the line?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Very good, we just noticed that coming up a little slow.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It sure is coming up slow, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And, Apollo 9, we are showing you about 60° yaw now; just wanted to mention that.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: That just about closes out my list here, I hit it briefly back there, unless you have any questions about my comments on that 2-degree pitch and yaw on the attitude for SPS-1.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Were you saying you were going to take the gimbal (garbled) off?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, you are really coming in scratchy here on this one, Apollo.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, I think we have it - I think we understand what you said.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And that cleans us up here, Apollo 9.
Have you got anything you would like to toss in here across this pad?
This is about the last time we plan on doing much talking to you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: No, I don't - I guess it is just the general comment we were pretty well crowded today to all of these things in, so we sort of missed lunch.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I could tell you were really humping up there.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We'd like to verify the canister change at 6:30.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It's in the process of being changed right now.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We are reading you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We have got you in here now.
Two other items; we would like to get an E memory dump from you to give us some homework here tonight if you can give us a Mark and take that.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Stand by, Apollo 9; our telemetry just dropped out.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We would like to know when you would like us to start charging the battery.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You can start it - You can start it any time prior to sacking out.
We are going to lose you here in about another minute and the only other time we will talk with you before sack time will be over Tananarive which will hit there at 24.
So, you can - You can start anytime you want.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Do you want that E-memory dump now or do you want to just skip it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, we are standing by now.
Go ahead and let her run.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It's - Houston it's (garbled) memory dump is on the way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And one other item, over Tananarive, if you can, we would like to have a PRD readout from each one of you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You want to check with - the panel 250 is configured right, and I'll start charging battery B up?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And we will see you over Tananarive at about 24 or 25.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Thank you, and we will get a PRD report as soon as we figure out what it is.
And we're charging battery B right now for you.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And that's a dosimeter reading over Tananarive.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Didn't say you wanted (garbled) or anything?
Look on the top, right there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Number 1, and the time you took it out was 07:49, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, how about the one we put in?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I ought to put a charge on B.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: 07:50:00 - start charge on BAT B; 2.25 amps and 83, 1.5.
Oh, no; hey, you know, this stuff (garbled) And 33 volts, 3 volts.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: There isn't a single (garbled); well, I'll be a son of a gun.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Here, let me take a peek.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, could you run a P50 and see if everything is normal there?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, our O flow's up again, finally.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Here it is right here, I think.
Is this one supposed to be number 3, that I have here?
Yes, here it is, marked right here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, I'm telling you we're going to get in trouble with those lithium hydroxide canisters.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I guess if he keeps marking them well, we may be able to avoid it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to go ahead and power down to G&N and stop worrying about getting in gimbal lock.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, Jim, look at the sun coming through the sextant.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You've got an eyeful of chocolate.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Isn't that amazing, that the LM held together?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Look at that O flow, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That O flow is working its way up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart
